title: exec redirect-only permanence (exec >file / 3>file / 2>&1 persist)
description:
Last path-2 piece (M5). exec with only redirects (no command word) must apply them permanently to the shell IO table for the rest of the session.

Root cause already diagnosed: Executor#with_redirects ensure closes the file opened over base (io.ios - base.ios), undoing exec's replace_io. The dup case (exec 2>&1) already works because no new stream is opened.

Fix: skip the close when the executor committed the io (io.equal?(@io) after replace_io). Verify bit-exact vs dash: exec >f / 3>f / 2>&1 / 9<f persist; subshell isolation (( exec >f ); after) does not leak; bad-path exec already fatal (7aj). One slice, green rake.

title: function-call redirects not applied to the function body
description: `f(){ echo body; }; f >file` — dash sends body to file; rush prints body to stdout. CommandRunner#run_function does not thread the redirected io into the function body (dispatch passes io to builtin/External but run_function ignores it). Likely fix: run the function body under executor.with_io(io). Found by the 7aj redirect fuzz; orthogonal to 7aj. Verify bit-exact vs dash + add to differential corpus.

title: shift past $# should abort the shell (special-builtin error)
description: `X=1; shift` with no positionals — dash: shift count exceeds $# is a special-builtin error → abort non-interactive shell with 2 (fires EXIT trap); rush no-ops with status 1. Reuse the BuiltinError fatal mechanism (7ab). Found by the 7aj fuzz; orthogonal. Verify vs dash + corpus.

title: low-value trio: hash / set -v / times
description: Not cleanly differential-testable: hash (path cache; awkward listing format), set -v (verbose, stderr-only), times (CPU times, non-deterministic). Can only check format/structure. Implement last in Phase 2.
